why: The signature AI inflation frame ('not just a tool, but a revolution'). Sets up a fake contrast to sound profound.
example: It's not just software, it's a revolution

why: Same elevation tic in negative form; classic LLM closing-paragraph move.
example: This isn't merely a trend, it's the future

term: 'it's not about X, it's about Y'
why: Pseudo-deep reframing template the model reaches for to manufacture insight.
example: It's not about the destination, it's about the journey

why: Lead-in to the elevation frame; high-frequency AI hype connective.
example: It's more than just a phone

why: The single most parodied AI essay opener.
example: In today's fast-paced digital world...

why: Movie-trailer opener the model uses to set false stakes.
example: In a world where attention is scarce...

pattern: \b(?:ever wondered|have you ever wondered|what if i told you|picture this|imagine a world|let that sink in)\b
why: Manufactured engagement opener; AI defaults to a rhetorical question to feign a hook.
example: Ever wondered why your code is slow?

why: Sweeping 'from A to B' range-claim that sounds comprehensive while saying little.
example: From startups to enterprises, everyone benefits

why: Fake-inclusive audience hedge; pads without committing.
example: Whether you're a beginner or a pro...

why: Filler hedge that announces nothing; one of the strongest AI prose tells.
example: It's important to note that results may vary

why: 'Dive/delve in' is a canonical ChatGPT transition.
example: Let's dive into the details

why: Marketing-AI staple; 'unlock' + abstract-noun is mode-collapse hype.
example: Unlock the power of your data

why: Empty escalation cliché.
example: Take your workflow to the next level

term: triadic negation 'No X. No Y. Just Z.'
why: Punchy negative-parallelism the model loves for taglines.
example: No fluff. No filler. Just results.

why: Faux-conversational pivot the model inserts for rhythm.
example: But here's the kicker...

why: Dead-metaphor summarizer.
example: At the end of the day, it's about people

why: Inflated novelty claim.
example: This is a game-changer

why: 'Navigate' + abstract noun is a studied ChatGPT focal-word pattern.
example: navigate the complexities of modern life

why: Verse Nurse flags 'with every ___' as a top Suno mad-lib opener; the blank is filler.
example: With every breath I take

pattern: \blost in (?:the |your )?(?:moment|music|night|rhythm|feeling|dark|stars|your eyes|the rain|the haze|the sound|the noise)\b
why: Default transcendence line with an interchangeable object.
example: Lost in the moment, lost in you

why: 'Chasing ___' aspiration mad-lib; one of Suno's most repeated frames.
example: Chasing dreams into the night

why: Archetypal generic Suno image ('dancing in the moonlight').
example: Dancing in the rain tonight

why: 'In the dead of night' and its variants are instant Suno tells.
example: In the dead of night

why: Default romantic set-dressing.
example: Under the stars we'll dance

why: Overwrought emotion-as-water cliché.
example: Drowning in my tears

why: Interiority padding the model adds to inflate a line.
example: Deep inside my soul

why: Passion-as-fire-in-the-body cliché; fully generic.
example: There's a fire in my soul

why: Dead-on-arrival passion image.
example: My heart's on fire

why: The canonical AI resilience trope (phoenix/ashes).
example: I rise from the ashes

why: Default freedom/liberation image.
example: Breaking free from these chains

why: Hope-in-darkness cliché.
example: You're my light in the dark

why: Top-tier love-song filler.
example: I'll never let you go

why: Union cliché.
example: Two hearts beat as one

why: Tell-don't-show emotion phrase.
example: I feel it in my soul

why: Tears/rain simile is maximally overused.
example: Tears falling like the rain

why: Default intimacy request.
example: Hold me close tonight

why: Generic party/passion hyperbole.
example: We'll set the night on fire

why: Resilience-through-adversity nature image.
example: A flower blooming through the cracks

why: Default vocable filler Suno drops in to pad a chorus.
example: Oh oh oh, na na na
